description: Anne Milton was Minister of State at the Department for Education from 12 June 2017 to 23 July 2019. She was elected as MP for Guildford (revised boundary) in 2010. Education Anne was educated at Haywards Heath Grammar School (which became Haywards Heath Sixth Form College in 1980, then Central Sussex College Sixth Form Campus in 2005) on Harlands Road in West Sussex. Political career Anne was appointed to the Health Select Committee, serving until December 2006, following her appointment as Shadow Minister for Tourism. In summer 2007 David Cameron appointed her Shadow Minister for Health. Between 2010 and 2012 she served as a Parliamentary Under-Secretary of State (Public Health), Department of Health and was appointed to the government as a whip with responsibility for HM Treasury and HM Household. Career outside politics Anne trained as a nurse at St Bartholomew’s Hospital in London and worked for the NHS for 25 years, as a district nurse and for people requiring palliative care.

details.body:
Anne Milton was Minister of State at the Department for Education from 12 June 2017 to 23 July 2019. She was elected as MP for Guildford (revised boundary) in 2010.

Education

Anne was educated at Haywards Heath Grammar School (which became Haywards Heath Sixth Form College in 1980, then Central Sussex College Sixth Form Campus in 2005) on Harlands Road in West Sussex.

Political career

Anne was appointed to the Health Select Committee, serving until December 2006, following her appointment as Shadow Minister for Tourism. In summer 2007 David Cameron appointed her Shadow Minister for Health. Between 2010 and 2012 she served as a Parliamentary Under-Secretary of State (Public Health), Department of Health and was appointed to the government as a whip with responsibility for HM Treasury and HM Household.

Career outside politics

Anne trained as a nurse at St Bartholomew’s Hospital in London and worked for the NHS for 25 years, as a district nurse and for people requiring palliative care.

Anne Milton was Minister of State at the Department for Education from 12 June 2017 to 23 July 2019. She was elected as MP for Guildford (revised boundary) in 2010.

Political career

Anne was appointed to the Health Select Committee, serving until December 2006, following her appointment as Shadow Minister for Tourism. The Minister for Women has overall responsibility for policy on gender equality.

role.title: Parliamentary Under-Secretary of State (Minister for Women)

role_appointments.title: The Rt Hon Anne Milton - Parliamentary Under Secretary of State (Minister for Women)

details.body:
Anne Milton was Minister of State at the Department for Education from 12 June 2017 to 23 July 2019. She was elected as MP for Guildford (revised boundary) in 2010.

Education

Anne was educated at Haywards Heath Grammar School (which became Haywards Heath Sixth Form College in 1980, then Central Sussex College Sixth Form Campus in 2005) on Harlands Road in West Sussex.

Political career

Anne was appointed to the Health Select Committee, serving until December 2006, following her appointment as Shadow Minister for Tourism. In summer 2007 David Cameron appointed her Shadow Minister for Health. Between 2010 and 2012 she served as a Parliamentary Under-Secretary of State (Public Health), Department of Health and was appointed to the government as a whip with responsibility for HM Treasury and HM Household.

Career outside politics

Anne trained as a nurse at St Bartholomew’s Hospital in London and worked for the NHS for 25 years, as a district nurse and for people requiring palliative care.

The minister’s responsibilities include:

  • apprenticeships including the apprenticeship levy, traineeships and institutes of technology
  • technical education and skills, including T-levels
  • careers education, information and guidance
  • post-16 funding (including support for young people and adult learners)
  • further education colleges, sixth-form colleges and local patterns of provision (including area reviews and city deals)
  • adult education, including the National Retraining Scheme and links to the review of post-18 education and funding
  • reducing the number of young people who are not in education, employment or training
  • the Department for Education non-executive board

role.title: Minister of State (Minister for Apprenticeships and Skills)

role_appointments.title: The Rt Hon Anne Milton - Minister of State for Apprenticeships and Skills

Whips are MPs or Lords appointed by each party to help organise their party’s contribution to parliamentary business. They are responsible for making sure the maximum number of their party members vote, and vote the way their party wants.

details.body:
Anne Milton was Minister of State at the Department for Education from 12 June 2017 to 23 July 2019. She was elected as MP for Guildford (revised boundary) in 2010.

Education

Anne was educated at Haywards Heath Grammar School (which became Haywards Heath Sixth Form College in 1980, then Central Sussex College Sixth Form Campus in 2005) on Harlands Road in West Sussex.

Political career

Anne was appointed to the Health Select Committee, serving until December 2006, following her appointment as Shadow Minister for Tourism. In summer 2007 David Cameron appointed her Shadow Minister for Health. Between 2010 and 2012 she served as a Parliamentary Under-Secretary of State (Public Health), Department of Health and was appointed to the government as a whip with responsibility for HM Treasury and HM Household.

Career outside politics

Anne trained as a nurse at St Bartholomew’s Hospital in London and worked for the NHS for 25 years, as a district nurse and for people requiring palliative care.

Ministerial responsibilities include:

  • antimicrobial resistance
  • cancer
  • dementia
  • diabetes
  • fertility and embryology
  • health improvement, including:
    • addiction to medicines
    • alcohol
    • drugs
    • obesity
    • physical activity
    • tobacco
  • health protection, including:
    • emergency preparedness
    • vaccination
  • long-term conditions
  • prevention measures, including:
    • children’s health and school nursing
    • fluoridation
    • health visiting
    • NHS healthchecks
    • preventing avoidable mortality
  • sexual health
